Ligusticum latifolium, also known as "Ligusticum wallichii", is a plant species in the Apiaceae family, commonly known as a type of medicinal ginger or Chinese chuanxiong. The health benefits of Ligusticum latifolium include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular protective effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, phenolic compounds, and volatile oils, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Ligusticum latifolium include teas, capsules, and decoctions, which are used to treat various conditions such as fever, headache, and digestive issues.
